title: Cannot pull or build images due to "Error: writing blob: storing blob to file '/var/tmp/storage813872647/1': happened during read: unexpected EOF"
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

Any attempt I make to pull an image results in this same error...
`Error: writing blob: storing blob to file "/var/tmp/storage813872647/1": happened during read: unexpected EOF`
I have tried looking into dozens of GitHub issues and tutorials on trying to fix the issue. Those that require setting `TMPDIR` looked promising but I have tried it and have gotten no-where. 

I am on MacOs.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman machine init -m 8192 --cpus 4 --disk-size=75`

2. `podman machine start`

3. `podman pull node:12-slim`


**Describe the results you received:**

I receive 
`Error: writing blob: storing blob to file "/var/tmp/storage813872647/1": happened during read: unexpected EOF`

**Describe the results you expected:**

I expect an image to be pulled. 

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

I am on MacOs on a company laptop. I have tried to look into how to change where the images are being stored and have gotten no-where. 

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version

Version:      3.4.4

Go Version:   go1.16.8
Built:        Wed Dec  8 16:45:07 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: It appears that pulling working behinds my company's Cisco VPN. Any workarounds for requiring the VPN to pull images?
That is up to you to setup the nextwork stack.  We have no control over that.
How could I configure the network stack properly to not require my VPN to be used?
There is nothing Podman can do. `unexpected EOF` can also happen with misconfigured proxies.

I have the same issue here, and in my case it seems like the registry is kicking me off for taking too long to fetch the image. I can't exactly speed it up though - the network isn't under my control.

What podman (or at least the underlying fetching code) _should_ be doing is resuming the download if the connection is broken (see fallacies of distributed computing #\1 "The network is reliable", #\3 "Bandwidth is infinite"). At the moment, though, it seems like it just dies after the first issue of any kind.

As @hakanai described, this is indeed true. I was trying to switch to podman from docker cli and realized the same issue while pulling images from my workplace's private container repo. I tried to pull a specific image numerous times and every time the pull fails with above mentioned error. I re-installed docker desktop and tried to repeat steps with docker CLI on the same hour of the day, same system, same network, same image and noticed that docker CLI was able to pull the image. However the important thing I noted was that docker kept retrying incrementally whenever there was an error, but eventually the pull succeeds, in more or less the same time it takes podman to notify that the pull failed. Also, pulls from public Docker Hub seems to be work just fine on the same system/network conditions using both docker CLI and podman CLI. So IMHO something definitely needs to improve with Podman.
@dpnishant would you open a new issue? If Docker works in the same environment, it really suggests Podman needs some more massaging in the retry semantics.

I encountered this issue today,  the image I pull is bigger than 1GB.  Everytime, podman pull failed to pull the image at the end with the error mentioned in this thread.  

tried multiple times, it seems doesn't using any cache from previous, and failed at the end. 

docker pull works
Please open a new issue.  We don't like to work on old closed issues, your problem might be different.
@weiwongfaye is there a new issue for this? i am having the same issue :(
